title: C# Basic — Paper 1
description: Foundational C# concepts: variables, strings, nullable types, type casting, operators, enums, structs, methods, ref/out, and classes.

questions.text: Which keyword is used to declare a constant in C# whose value cannot be changed at runtime?
What is the default value of an uninitialized `bool` field in a C# class?
Which C# data type is best suited for storing monetary values to avoid floating-point rounding errors?
Which method extracts a portion of a string starting at a given index with a specified length?
What is the result of `string.Compare("abc", "ABC", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)`?
What does `int? x = null;` declare in C#?

options.text: A regular integer set to zero
A nullable integer that can hold null

questions.text: What is the result of `int? a = null; int b = a ?? 10;`?
Given `string? s = null;`, what does `int len = s?.Length ?? -1;` evaluate to?
Which of the following correctly performs an explicit cast from `double` to `int` in C#?
What does `int.TryParse("abc", out int result)` return?
What is boxing in C#?

options.text: Converting a reference type to a value type
Converting a value type to an object reference type
Wrapping a class inside another class
Converting a string to a numeric type

questions.text: What is the result of `10 % 3` in C#?
Which operator is the ternary conditional operator in C#?
What is the result of `true && false || true` in C#?
How do you declare an enum named `Direction` with values North, South, East, West in C#?

options.text: enum Direction { North, South, East, West }
struct Direction { North, South, East, West }
class Direction : Enum { North, South, East, West }
define Direction = { North, South, East, West }

questions.text: By default, what is the underlying type of a C# enum?
What attribute allows a C# enum to be used as a bit flag, enabling bitwise combinations?
In C#, a `struct` is a value type. What does this mean regarding assignment?

options.text: Both variables point to the same memory location after assignment
A copy of the struct is made when assigned to another variable
Structs cannot be assigned to variables
Structs are always allocated on the heap

questions.text: Which statement is TRUE about structs vs classes in C#?

options.text: Structs support inheritance from other structs
Structs can have parameterless constructors in C# 10+
Structs are always slower than classes

questions.text: What is the purpose of the `params` keyword in a C# method signature?

options.text: It marks a parameter as required
It allows the method to accept a variable number of arguments
It passes a parameter by reference
It makes a parameter optional with a default value

questions.text: Which of the following demonstrates method overloading in C#?

options.text: Two methods with the same name and same parameter types in the same class
Two methods with the same name but different parameter types in the same class
A method in a derived class with the same name as in the base class
Two methods with different names but the same body

questions.text: What is the output of calling `Greet(greeting: "Hi", name: "Alice")` if the method signature is `void Greet(string name, string greeting)`?

options.text: Compilation error — named arguments must match order
The call is valid; named arguments can be in any order

questions.text: What is the key difference between `ref` and `out` parameters in C#?

options.text: ref requires the variable to be initialized before the call; out does not
out requires the variable to be initialized before the call; ref does not
ref and out are identical in behavior
ref is only for value types; out is only for reference types

questions.text: What does the `out` keyword guarantee about a parameter inside the called method?

options.text: It must be read before being written
It must be assigned a value before the method returns
It is passed as a copy

questions.text: When should you use the `in` parameter modifier in C#?

options.text: When you want the method to modify the original variable
When you want to pass a value type by reference but prevent modification
When returning multiple values from a method
When using iterator methods with yield

questions.text: What does the `new` keyword do when used with a class in C#?

options.text: It declares a new variable type
It allocates memory and calls the constructor to create an object instance
It creates a value-type copy of an existing object

questions.text: What is object initializer syntax in C#?

options.text: A way to declare and allocate arrays inline
Setting properties of an object at the time of creation without a special constructor
Calling the constructor with named arguments
A pattern for factory method creation

questions.text: Which of the following correctly uses object initializer syntax to create a `Person` with Name "Alice" and Age 30?

options.text: var p = new Person { Name = "Alice", Age = 30 };

questions.text: What is a field in a C# class?

options.text: A method that returns a value
A variable declared directly inside a class body that stores state
